title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.imageAlt: A bowl of creamy, rich authentic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani served with naan bread and rice.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, aromatic flavors of homemade Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani, a classic Indian delight.

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a beloved Indian classic renowned for its rich, creamy tomato-based sauce. Tender pieces of marinated chicken are simmered to perfection in a luscious gravy, offering a harmonious blend of tangy, sweet, and mildly spicy flavors. This iconic dish is a comforting and indulgent treat, perfect for any special occasion or a satisfying weekday meal.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 min

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with plain yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, 1 teaspoon red chili powder, turmeric powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, and salt. Mix well to ensure all chicken pieces are coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a large pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Sear the chicken for 3-4 minutes per side until lightly browned and cooked through. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Makhani Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, melt the remaining 3 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the remaining 2 tablespoons ginger-garlic paste and green chilies (if using), cooking for another minute until fragrant. Add the crushed tomatoes, Kashmiri red chili powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, and garam masala. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 10-12 minutes until the tomatoes break down and the mixture thickens, and oil starts separating from the sides.

instructions.title: Blend the Gravy
instructions.text: While the gravy base is cooking, blend the soaked cashews with 1/2 cup of water until a smooth paste is formed. Once the tomato mixture is cooked, carefully transfer it to a blender along with the cashew paste. Blend until perfectly smooth. For an extra silky texture, pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve back into the clean pan.

instructions.title: Combine Chicken and Gravy
instructions.text: Return the smooth gravy to the pan over medium-low heat. Add sugar and salt to taste, adjusting for your preference. Stir in the cooked chicken pieces, ensuring they are fully coated in the rich gravy. Simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the gravy.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in the crushed Kasuri Methi and the heavy cream. Gently warm through for 1-2 minutes; avoid boiling after adding cream. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Garnish generously with fresh chopped coriander leaves. Serve hot with naan bread, roti, or steamed basmati rice.

tips: For a deeper flavor, marinate the chicken overnight. This allows the spices to fully penetrate the meat, making it incredibly tender and flavorful.
Always use good quality canned crushed tomatoes or fresh ripe tomatoes for the gravy base. Kashmiri red chili powder is recommended for its vibrant color without adding excessive heat.
Ensure the gravy is blended until perfectly smooth. A fine-mesh sieve can be used after blending to remove any remaining solids for a truly restaurant-style silky texture.
article:
Mastering the Art of Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani): Your Ultimate Guide to an Indian Lunch Classic

Few dishes capture the heart and palate quite like Butter Chicken, known in Hindi as Murgh Makhani. This iconic Indian curry, with its rich, creamy tomato gravy and succulent pieces of chicken, is a global sensation for a reason. It’s a dish that embodies comfort, luxury, and a symphony of flavors, making it a perfect choice for a satisfying lunch or a special dinner. If you’ve ever dreamed of recreating that restaurant-quality taste in your own kitchen, you’ve come to the right place. This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know to prepare an authentic and utterly delicious Butter Chicken that will impress even the most discerning palates.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, literally translates to "chicken with butter." Originating in Delhi, India, in the 1950s, it was reportedly created by Kundan Lal Gujral at Moti Mahal restaurant. The story goes that he used leftover tandoori chicken by simmering it in a rich tomato gravy, thus inventing one of the world's most beloved curries. At its core, Butter Chicken features tender chicken, often marinated in yogurt and spices and then grilled or pan-fried, submerged in a velvety smooth sauce. This sauce is a marvel of taste and texture, typically made from tomatoes, butter (lots of it!), cream, cashews or almonds for thickness, and a blend of aromatic spices like garam masala, fenugreek (kasoori methi), and ginger-garlic paste. The result is a dish that is mildly spicy, sweet, tangy, and incredibly rich, making it incredibly appealing to a wide range of tastes, including those new to Indian cuisine. It’s a staple in Indian restaurants worldwide and a cherished home-cooked meal, representing the pinnacle of comfort food.

## Ingredients You Need for Butter Chicken

Creating the perfect Butter Chicken requires a careful selection of ingredients for both the chicken marinade and the exquisite sauce.

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri for color, or regular for heat)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1/2 teaspoon garam masala
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   1 tablespoon lemon juice

For the Makhani (Butter) Sauce:
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter (and more for finishing!)
*   1 tablespoon oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 teaspoon ground cumin
*   1/2 teaspoon ground coriander
*   1 (800g) can crushed tomatoes or 4-5 ripe tomatoes, pureed
*   1/4 cup cashews (soaked in warm water for 15 minutes, then drained) or 2 tablespoons almond flour
*   1/2 cup water or chicken broth
*   1 teaspoon sugar (or to taste)
*   1/2 teaspoon salt (or to taste)
*   1/4 cup heavy cream (plus more for garnish)
*   1 teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi), crushed
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped (for garnish)

## Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Butter Chicken

Follow these steps for a truly memorable Butter Chicken experience:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well, ensuring the chicken is thoroughly coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, or even overnight for maximum flavor.
2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 3-5 minutes per side, until lightly charred and cooked through. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.
3.  **Prepare the Sauce Base:** In the same pan, add the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter and 1 tablespoon of oil. Once melted, add the chopped onions and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes.
4.  **Add Aromatics and Spices:** Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Add the red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, ground cumin, and ground coriander. Sauté for another minute, stirring constantly, until the spices are fragrant. Be careful not to burn them.
5.  **Simmer the Sauce:** Pour in the crushed tomatoes (or tomato puree), soaked cashews (or almond flour), water/broth, sugar, and salt. Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken slightly.
6.  **Blend the Sauce:** Carefully transfer the sauce mixture to a blender (or use an immersion blender directly in the pot). Blend until completely smooth and creamy. For an extra silky sauce, you can strain it through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan.
7.  **Combine and Finish:** Return the smooth sauce to the pan over low heat. Stir in the cooked chicken pieces. Add the heavy cream and crushed kasoori methi. Simmer for another 5-10 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the flavors and the sauce to warm through. Taste and adjust salt or sugar if needed.
8.  **Garnish and Serve:** Garnish with a swirl of extra heavy cream, a knob of butter, and fresh chopped cilantro.

## Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken

Achieving restaurant-quality Butter Chicken at home is entirely possible with a few expert tricks:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight is best.
*   **Char the Chicken:** While you don't need a tandoor oven, getting a slight char on the chicken pieces adds a wonderful smoky depth that mimics traditional tandoori chicken. A hot skillet or even a quick broil in the oven works wonders.
*   **Blend Until Smooth:** For that signature velvety texture, blend the sauce thoroughly. If you have a powerful blender, you might not need to strain it, but straining ensures no fibrous bits remain.
*   **Kasoori Methi (Dried Fenugreek Leaves):** This ingredient is non-negotiable for authentic Butter Chicken flavor. Crush it between your palms before adding to release its aroma. It adds a slightly bitter, earthy, and sweet undertone that defines the dish.
*   **Don't Fear the Butter and Cream:** It’s called Butter Chicken for a reason! These ingredients contribute significantly to the richness and luxuriousness of the sauce. Adjust to your preference, but don't hold back too much if you want an authentic taste.
*   **Balance of Flavors:** Taste your sauce frequently. The perfect Butter Chicken strikes a balance between sweet, tangy, and savory. Adjust sugar, salt, or even a tiny squeeze of lemon juice at the end to achieve this.

## Serving Suggestions for Murgh Makhani

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs beautifully with a variety of accompaniments, making it a truly satisfying Indian lunch or dinner.

*   **Basmati Rice:** Steamed basmati rice is the classic companion, perfect for soaking up every last drop of the rich, creamy sauce.
*   **Naan Bread:** Warm, fluffy naan bread (plain, garlic, or butter naan) is another essential. Tear off pieces and use them to scoop up the chicken and sauce.
*   **Roti or Chapati:** For a lighter option, whole wheat roti or chapati also works wonderfully.
*   **Cucumber Raita:** A cooling yogurt-based side dish with grated cucumber and a hint of spice provides a refreshing contrast to the richness of the curry.
*   **Simple Salad:** A fresh green salad with a light vinaigrette can also cut through the richness and add a fresh element to your meal.
*   **Pickle and Pappadums:** Indian pickles (achar) and crispy pappadums can add an extra layer of texture and zing to your spread.

## Variations and Substitutions for Butter Chicken

While the classic Butter Chicken recipe is divine, there are many ways to adapt it to dietary needs or personal preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Butter "Chicken":** Replace the chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), tofu, or mixed vegetables like cauliflower and potatoes. Marinate and pan-fry the paneer or tofu before adding it to the sauce.
*   **Vegan Option:** Use plant-based yogurt for the marinade, swap butter for vegan butter, and heavy cream for full-fat coconut milk or cashew cream. Ensure all other ingredients are vegan-friendly.
*   **Spicy Kick:** For those who prefer more heat, increase the amount of red chili powder or add a finely chopped green chili during the sautéing of the onions.
*   **Healthier Version:** Reduce the amount of butter and cream, or use low-fat dairy alternatives. You can also increase the vegetable content in the sauce (e.g., add carrots or bell peppers) to boost nutrients.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** If you want a more pronounced smoky flavor without a tandoor, after cooking the chicken, you can perform a "dhungar" or smoke infusion. Place a small piece of burning charcoal in a small bowl in the center of the pot, drizzle a teaspoon of ghee or oil over it, and quickly cover the pot for a few minutes.
*   **Nut-Free Option:** If you have a nut allergy, you can omit the cashews. The sauce might be slightly thinner, but still delicious. You can use a tablespoon of gram flour (besan) roasted in the pan for a minute to thicken the sauce if desired.

Butter Chicken is more than just a dish; it’s an experience. With this guide, you’re well-equipped to bring the authentic flavors of Murgh Makhani to your table, creating a memorable Indian lunch or dinner that celebrates the rich culinary heritage of India. Enjoy the process, savor the aromas, and delight in every creamy, flavorful bite!
